text: When building a website, security is essential to protect both users and data. One basic step is using HTTPS, which encrypts information exchanged between the browser and the server. This prevents attackers from intercepting sensitive details like passwords. Authentication is another key aspect. Strong login systems, such as requiring complex passwords or two-factor authentication, help verify user identity. Additionally, input validation is crucial. Developers must check all data submitted by users to prevent harmful code from being executed. Common vulnerabilities, such as SQL injection or cross-site scripting, can compromise a site. By validating inputs and keeping software updated, these risks are greatly reduced.
